conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out! I can help you determine your Child Benefit eligibility. To assess this, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the basics: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. I'm asking about both of them - Alex who is 8 years old, and Blake who is 14.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (8 years old) and Blake (14 years old).

Let me ask you the next question: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! Now I need to understand your relationship with the children.

**Do Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to check a few more things to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to ensure I have all the information I need.

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital or care or anything like that. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £60,000 per year?**

(Note: This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you need to pay a tax charge if your income is over £60,000, or you might choose not to receive the payments if income is over £80,000.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earn over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information! I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

**For Alex (8 years old):** You meet all the eligibility criteria. Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**For Blake (14 years old):** You also meet all the eligibility criteria. Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

Since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you won't need to worry about the High Income Child Benefit tax charge either.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for both children. Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, but you can claim for multiple children on the same application. If you haven't already claimed, you should apply as soon as possible, as payments can usually only be backdated for up to 3 months.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
